erd.title: ERD (Effective Rim Diameter)
erd.description:
The diameter measured between the points where the spoke nipples seat on the rim — i.e., the effective circle the spoke ends form.

This is NOT the rim's outer or bead diameter. Most rim makers publish the ERD; if not, measure it with a ruler or dedicated ERD gauge.

Unit: mm.

pcd.title: PCD (Pitch Circle Diameter)
pcd.description:
The diameter of the circle passing through the centers of the spoke holes on the hub flange.

Left and Right flanges often have different PCDs (especially on rear hubs and disc-brake hubs), so measure each side separately.

Unit: mm.

flangeDistance.title: Flange Distance
flangeDistance.description:
The horizontal distance from the hub's center plane (centerline of the locknuts) to each flange.

Left and Right are usually asymmetric due to cassette / disc rotor offset — measure each side independently.

Unit: mm.

spokeHoleDiameter.title: Spoke Hole Diameter
spokeHoleDiameter.description:
The diameter of the spoke holes on the hub flange. Used to correct the spoke length so the spoke reaches across the hole rather than its center.

Typically around 2.5 mm for standard hubs.

Unit: mm.

crossings.title: Crossings
crossings.description:
The number of times each spoke crosses other spokes between the hub and the rim.

0 = radial (straight from hub to rim).
2 or 3 cross is most common; 4 cross is used on some high-load builds.

Left and Right can use different crossing counts.
